title: Fagelia perfoliata
taxonomy: Fagelia perfoliata is a flowering plant species. It belongs to the family Asteraceae. The genus Fagelia is a part of the tribe Mutisieae. Fagelia perfoliata is native to the Andean region. Its classification is within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Fagelia perfoliata, also known as sweet-scented bedstraw, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion. They have also been employed to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant has been used as a mild diuretic to help with urinary issues. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses.

Additionally, Fagelia perfoliata has been used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and coughs.
